matches.match_rationale: The core pain is that mismatches between HR records and payroll ledger — duplicate entries, missing staff, wrong salary grades — go undetected until after payroll runs. Anomaly detection directly addresses this by flagging rows that deviate from expected patterns (e.g., salary grade inconsistent with role level, headcount mismatch between systems, or a staff ID appearing twice). For a 500+ staff multi-site operator, these discrepancies follow detectable statistical signatures that a trained model can surface pre-run.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A pre-payroll anomaly scan that ingests the current payroll file and HR master data, flags rows where headcount, salary grade, or employment status diverge from expected norms, and produces a reconciliation exception report for payroll staff to clear before processing.

matches.match_rationale: HR systems and payroll ledgers often use different schemas, field naming conventions, and status codes — the reconciliation failure is partly a data mapping problem. AI-assisted ETL can normalize headcount exports from an HR system (e.g., SAP, Workday) into the target schema expected by payroll, mapping role transitions, salary grade codes, and employment status flags consistently. This directly reduces the manual effort of aligning semi-structured HR data to payroll input format before each run.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated pre-payroll pipeline that ingests HR master data exports, standardizes field mappings and status codes, and outputs a clean, payroll-ready file with a diff report showing all changes since the last run.

matches.match_rationale: Payroll-HR reconciliation is a multi-step workflow: pull HR master data, pull payroll ledger, diff the two on headcount and salary grades, flag exceptions, route exceptions for approval, and confirm clearance before payroll runs. This is precisely the pattern task automation agents address — multi-step, cross-system, decision-branching workflows that today require a payroll officer to manually execute each step. The 6-12 month feasibility is strong because the workflow is well-defined and the data sources are structured.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An autonomous reconciliation agent that runs on a scheduled trigger before each payroll cycle, diffs HR and payroll data across all sites, routes exception cases to the responsible HR manager for sign-off via email or chat, and locks the payroll run until all exceptions are cleared.

matches.match_rationale: After reconciliation exceptions are identified, payroll teams need to communicate discrepancies to HR managers across multiple sites — writing exception summaries, correction requests, and audit trail narratives. LLM text generation can auto-draft these communications and formal reconciliation reports from structured diff data, reducing the manual write-up burden that compounds the overall time cost of reconciliation.
matches.opportunity_sketch: Auto-generated exception notification emails and monthly payroll reconciliation summary reports drafted from structured diff outputs, ready for payroll officer review and send.
